page: Microsoft Azure E64s_v5 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The configuration delivers 64 vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of memory per vCPU. This instance does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, relying instead on remote Premium Storage options.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with large-block read and write speeds reaching 296.85 GB/sec and 136.92 GB/sec respectively, both ranking in the top tier. The system demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations, achieving 62.15 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and scoring highly in Passmark database operations. Single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, remains in the average tier.

Due to the lack of dedicated GPU hardware, the server exhibits weak performance in large-scale LLM text generation, specifically scoring in the bottom 25% for 70B model text generation at 2.73 tokens/sec. Additionally, the absence of local storage requires architectural reliance on network-attached storage. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory density and multi-core CPU scaling without GPU requirements. It is highly suited for memory-intensive database deployments, Redis caching layers, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized virtual machine within the ESv5 family, running on Microsoft Hyper-V. It is configured with 64 vCPUs across 32 physical Intel Xeon 8370C cores running at 2.8 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, requiring remote Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with top-tier results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. However, single-core metrics are average, and the lack of a GPU results in weak text generation speeds for large LLM models. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density memory workloads, making it suitable for large databases, Redis caching, multi-threaded software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v5 is an ESv5-family virtual machine featuring 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB of RAM. It delivers strong multi-core and database performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v5 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring sixty-four vCPUs and half a terabyte of RAM for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores
512 GB of RAM providing a high 8:1 memory-to-vCPU ratio
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Optimized for remote Premium Storage with no local disk space
Best suited for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded compilation
